cards.title: Code Golf
cards.body: Code golf is a challenge to write the shortest possible code to achieve a task, often used to demonstrate programming skills.

cards.title: COBOL
cards.body: COBOL, or Common Business-Oriented Language, is a high-level programming language first developed in 1959.

cards.title: Linux Kernel
cards.body: The Linux kernel is the core of the Linux operating system, written by Linus Torvalds in 1991.

cards.title: Buffer Overflow
cards.body: A buffer overflow occurs when a program writes data to a buffer, exceeding its capacity, potentially causing a crash or security vulnerability.

cards.title: John McCarthy
cards.body: John McCarthy, an American computer scientist, coined the term 'Artificial Intelligence' in 1956.

cards.title: Y2K Bug
cards.body: The Y2K bug, or Millennium Bug, was a widespread concern that computer systems would fail when the year 2000 began.

cards.title: SQL Injection
cards.body: SQL injection is a type of security vulnerability that occurs when an attacker injects malicious SQL code into a web application.

cards.title: Donald Knuth
cards.body: Donald Knuth, an American computer scientist, wrote the comprehensive book series 'The Art of Computer Programming'.

cards.title: Phreaking
cards.body: Phreaking, or phone phreaking, is the exploration and experimentation with telephone systems.

cards.title: CAPTCHA
cards.body: A CAPTCHA, or Completely Automated Public Turing test, is a challenge to determine whether the user is human or a computer.

quizzes.question: What is the goal of Code Golf?
options: To write the most complex code
To write the most readable code
quizzes.explanation: The goal of Code Golf is to write the shortest possible code to achieve a task.

quizzes.question: Who wrote the Linux kernel?
quizzes.explanation: Linus Torvalds wrote the Linux kernel in 1991.

quizzes.question: What is SQL injection?
quizzes.explanation: SQL injection is a type of security vulnerability that occurs when an attacker injects malicious SQL code into a web application.
